title: Frozen Nematode Sample Preparation for Sequencing
author: Manuela Kieninger

steps.title: Put an equal number of adult...
steps.instruction: Put an equal number of adult females or hermaphrodites containing embryos on 9 cm NGM plates seeded with food bacteria. I usually put 20 worms on each plate and I prepare about 20 plates.

steps.title: Chill a centrifuge which can accommodate...
steps.instruction: Chill a centrifuge which can accommodate 50 ml and 15 ml tubes down to 4°C

steps.title: Move worm pellet in 15 ml...
steps.instruction: Move worm pellet in 15 ml falcon tube and add cold (fridge temperature) M9 plus 0.01% Tween to a final volume of 14 ml.

steps.title: Perform a final wash using PBS
steps.instruction: Perform a final wash using PBS. Fill the tube with PBS to a final volume of 14 ml and spin 2 min @ 800 rcf. You now have clean worms ready to make pellets for freezing.

steps.title: Seal the plates with Parafilm
steps.instruction: Seal the plates with Parafilm. Put the plates in a box and incubate the box at the respective growing temperature for the nematode species.

steps.title: Let the nematodes grow until bacteria...
steps.instruction: Let the nematodes grow until bacteria is gone or the plate is full of nematodes. The nematodes should not be starved!

steps.title: Add a small amount of fridge-chilled M9 to each plate
steps.instruction: Add a small amount of fridge-chilled M9 to each plate. Gently swirl to release the worms, and carefully pour into a 50 ml Falcon tube (if you have many plates, you can add M9 to the first plate, pour it into the second plate, and so on. So you do not end up with a large volume.) Keep the falcon tube on ice to keep the solution cool.

steps.title: Repeat the washing step until most...
steps.instruction: Repeat the washing step until most of the worms have been washed off. Discard the washed plates.

steps.title: Fill the falcon tube(s) up to...
steps.instruction: Fill the falcon tube(s) up to 40 ml with additional M9 and invert the falcon tube(s) several times.

steps.title: Spin tubes at 2500 rcf for 8 mins at 4ºC
steps.instruction: Spin tubes at 2500 rcf for 8 mins at 4ºC. ( Important: Different species might settle at different forces. You might need to go higher with the g-force if you see still a lot of worms floating after the centrifugation.)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant with a transfer pipet
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant with a transfer pipet. Take care not to disturb the worm pellet.

steps.title: Fill the falcon tube(s) back up...
steps.instruction: Fill the falcon tube(s) back up with chilled M9 supplemented with 0.01% Tween.

steps.title: Spin tubes @ 2500 rcf for 8 mins @ 4ºC
steps.instruction: Spin tubes @ 2500 rcf for 8 mins @ 4ºC.

steps.title: Take off the supernatant
steps.instruction: Take off the supernatant.

steps.title: If the supernatant is clear go...
steps.instruction: If the supernatant is clear go to the Sucrose float step (14). If you still have a lot of bacteria in the supernatant wash again with M9/Tween until the supernatant is clear (7-12).

steps.title: Spin down for 2 min @ 800 rcf
steps.instruction: Spin down for 2 min @ 800 rcf.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and keep the tubes on ice
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and keep the tubes on ice. If you have more than 2 ml worm pellet, divide the worms in more 15 ml tubes. Then top up to 5 ml total volume with cold M9/Tween.

steps.title: Immediately spin the tube(s) for 5...
steps.instruction: Immediately spin the tube(s) for 5 min @ 3500 rcf @ 4°C. The "clean" worm should float to the top of the liquid and the bacteria and other contaminations are at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Take off the floating worms with washed transfer pipet
steps.instruction: Take off the floating worms with washed transfer pipet. Put them in a new 15 ml falcon tube.

steps.title: Wash a new transfer pipet with M9/Tween
steps.instruction: Wash a new transfer pipet with M9/Tween. If you are not washing the tip before, the worms will stick to the tip and you will loose a lot of your sample!

steps.title: Wash twice with M9/Tween
steps.instruction: Wash twice with M9/Tween: Fill the tube with M9/Tween to 14 ml final volume and spin 2 min @ 800 rcf in a cold centrifuge (4°C).

steps.title: Add about 70 to 80 µl of worms in 1
steps.instruction: Add about 70 to 80 µl of worms in 1.5 ml tubes (can be pestle tubes used in the Monarch HMW DNA extraction kit for tissue from NEB in case you plan HMW DNA extractions). Make sure you add the worms to the bottom of the tube. Note: I like to use a wide-orifice 200 µl tip here which is low bind.

steps.title: After flash freezing in liquid nitrogen,...
steps.instruction: After flash freezing in liquid nitrogen, take the tubes with the frozen nematodes out using the long tweezers and immediately store the tubes in a ULT (ultra low temperature) freezer.

steps.title: Use low-bind tip to transfer worms...
steps.instruction: Use low-bind tip to transfer worms and wash the tip in M9/Tween before using.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant. Add 1-2 ml PBS and transfer all of the worms in 1.5 ml or 2 ml LoBind tube(s). I would only fill them half. Use another tube if you have a lot of sample.

steps.title: Spin the worms down for 1 min @ 10
steps.instruction: Spin the worms down for 1 min @ 10.000 rcf (the centrifuge can be at room temperature here).

steps.title: If you want to know the...
steps.instruction: If you want to know the exact weight of your sample, you can weigh the empty tubes before and weigh them again after addition of worms. I usually add about 70 to 80 µl which comes to at least 60 mg of worms. I avoid to have more than 100 mg in one tube.

steps.title: Using long tweezers of about 30 cm, lower the 1
steps.instruction: Using long tweezers of about 30 cm, lower the 1.5 ml tubes with nematodes in liquid nitrogen and let them float in there for a bit. It is important to lower them with tweezers so the sample does not splash all over the tube.

steps.title: Get your PPE for liquid nitrogen handling
steps.instruction: Get your PPE for liquid nitrogen handling. Fill a small dewar for liquid nitrogen with a bit liquid nitrogen for flash freezing the tubes with the nematodes.

steps.title: Add 5 ml cold 60% (w/v) Sucrose
steps.instruction: Add 5 ml cold 60% (w/v) Sucrose.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: If you had a lot of contamination in your washed worms, it might be necessary to repeat the Sucrose wash.
